element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Strategic foundation - players randomly select leader stats for their character and successors before gameplay begins
Players inherit starting territories based on faction choice (Mongols, Byzantine, England, Japan)

element_introduction_order.element: Turn-based action economy (3 choices per turn)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn to allocate limited actions: troop training, merchant transactions, drafing soldiers, treaties, or warfare
Buying products/quantities from merchants; managing economy stability as prerequisite to military action
Drafting soldiers and training troops required before conquest attempts
Seasons govern troop payment schedules, crop harvests, food distribution - creates temporal pressure layers
Alternative to warfare; uses one of three action choices
Requires preparation (economy stability, trained army); consumes one action choice; primary endgame objective

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Stated as core mechanic but integration timing unclear
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Arranging marriages, fathering children, appointing family to government positions for long-term dynasty control

typical_tension_triggers: Limited action choices (3 per turn) forcing prioritization
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based macro strategy with cyclical seasonal pressure; tension builds across seasons as resources deplete and rivals strengthen; relief comes through successful economic management and harvest cycles

difficulty_curve.early_game: Learning curve steep due to resource management and action economy constraints; starting with limited military options and economic fragility
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Established economies allow military expansion; rival powers consolidate; seasonal cycles create predictable but challenging pressure
difficulty_curve.late_game: Conquest of remaining territories requires careful balancing of military, economic, and diplomatic resources; increasing complexity as faction powers grow
notable_spikes: First inter-faction conflict when military is insufficient
Seasonal payment obligations when treasury is low
Coordinated rival attacks in multiplayer modes

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player historical progression mode beginning 1175 A.D. Player controls Lord Temujin with objective to conquer the Old World. Emphasizes narrative historical progression and economy-before-military sequencing.

Multi-faction mode (1-4 players) beginning 1206 A.D. Supports faction selection (Mongols, Byzantine, England, Japan). Each country cycles through turns in rotating order; seasons reset cycle order each cycle. Creates competitive pressure through turn order variation.

Recurring macro-rhythm where seasons gate specific events (troop payment, harvest, distribution). Creates predictable yet constraining temporal structure forcing strategic planning across multiple turns.
